# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { openKv } from "https://deno.land/std@0.134.0/dotenv/mod.ts";
import { Client as KvClient } from 'https://cdn.skimovies.com/denovk/index.js';

const kv = await openKv({
  name: "my_kvs",
});

async function searchUsers(req: Request) {
  const query = new URLSearchParams(req.url.split("?")[1]).get("q");
  if (!query) return { status: 400, body: "Missing 'q' parameter" };

  const filterParam = req.url.includes('?') ? req.url.split('?')[1] : "";
  const params = new URLSearchParams(filterParam);

  const userIds = await kv.keys(['users', '*', '*']);

  for (const key of userIds) {
    try {
      const user = await kv.get(key);
      if (user.name.toLowerCase().includes(query!.toLowerCase()) || 
        user.email.toLowerCase().includes(query!.toLowerCase())) {

        return { body: JSON.stringify(user), status: 200 };
      }
    } catch (error) {
      console.error(error);
    }
  }

  return { status: 404, body: "User not found" };
}

async function main() {
  const req = await fetch(new URL("/api/users", import.meta.url));
  const res = await searchUsers(req);
  console.log(res.body);
}

main();
